evals.prompt: I'm cherry-picking commit abc1234 and have a conflict in src/Services/UserService.php. Walk me through resolving it.
evals.expected_output: Instructs to: (1) run git show abc1234 --stat to get the exact line-count summary, (2) run git show abc1234 -- src/Services/UserService.php to read every + and - line, (3) read the current HEAD state of the conflicted region, (4) apply only those +/- lines onto HEAD's structure without restructuring, (5) verify the staged diff count matches step 1, (6) stop and wait for user approval before running git cherry-pick --continue.
assertions: Starts with git show <hash> --stat to get the line-count summary
Reads git show <hash> -- <file> as the authoritative source of changes
Explicitly warns against relying solely on conflict markers
Instructs to verify staged diff count matches the summary
Does not run git commit or git cherry-pick --continue autonomously

evals.prompt: The conflict markers show a method in the incoming (>>>>>>>) side that already exists at a different line in the file. What should I do?
evals.expected_output: Discard the incoming side entirely — the method was relocated in HEAD, not removed. Do not duplicate it. Instead, find where it currently lives and apply only the specific +/- lines from the cherry-picked commit there.
assertions: Advises discarding the incoming conflict side
Explains the method was moved, not removed
Warns against duplicating the existing method
Directs to apply the commit's changes where the method currently lives in HEAD

evals.prompt: I've resolved the conflict and staged the file. Can I run git cherry-pick --continue now?
evals.expected_output: Not yet — first run git diff --staged -- <file> and verify the line count matches what git show <hash> --stat reported. Only after confirming ask the user for explicit approval before continuing.
assertions: Instructs to run git diff --staged to verify the diff count
Requires the count to match the original git show --stat summary
Does not run git cherry-pick --continue autonomously
Asks for user confirmation before proceeding
